TCA-IRR
Back to Help & Support

Guide: Analyst Analysis & Manual Input

Learn how to add qualitative insights and run deeper analysis as a Analyst.

Performing a Manual Review
This guide explains how to use the "Analyst Analysis" page to enrich the AI's evaluation.
1

Start a Review from the Hub

Navigate to the 'Analyst Hub' from the sidebar. Find a 'Pending Review' assignment and click 'Start Review'. This will take you to the main Analyst Analysis page.

Go to Analyst Hub
2

Submit All Relevant Documents

Use the 'Document Submission' section to upload any files (transcripts, reports), import from URLs, or paste raw text. This contextual information is crucial for both your manual review and the AI's deep analysis.

3

Add Qualitative Insights

In the 'Qualitative Insights' section, add your notes from CEO interviews, general observations, or any deep analysis you've conducted. This is where your human expertise provides critical context.

4

Provide Specific Comments for NLP Analysis

Use the 'NLP Analysis Input' section to add 30-40 specific, distinct comments. These comments will be used for thematic analysis, sentiment scoring, and to compare your findings against the AI's (AI vs. Human Gap Analysis).

5

Save and Run Full Analysis

First, save your qualitative inputs using the 'Save Analyst Analysis' button. Then, click 'Run Full Analyst Analysis'. This triggers the deep NLP analysis on your comments and generates the final comparison charts.

6

Incorporate into the Final Report

Once the analysis is complete, the results (Thematic Analysis, AI-Human Deviation, etc.) will be displayed on the page. These insights will also be automatically included in the final Due Diligence report for this company.
